title: The Little Old Cupid
content:
'Twas a very small garden;
    The paths were of stone,
    Scattered with leaves,
    With moss overgrown;
    And a little old Cupid
    Stood under a tree,
    With a small broken bow
    He stood aiming at me.

    The dog-rose in briars
    Hung over the weeds,
    The air was aflock
    With the floating of seed,
    And a little old Cupid
    Stood under a tree,
    With a small broken bow
    He stood aiming at me.

    The dovecote was tumbling,
    The fountain dry,
    A wind in the orchard
    Went whispering by;
    And a little old Cupid
    Stood under a tree,
    With a small broken bow
    He stood aiming at me.
meaning:
"The Little Old Cupid" is a quintessential example of Walter De La Mare's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
